hostManage.descUserGroupPre: Manage system users, groups, and roles on
hostManage.descUserGroupPost: Create, modify, and delete user accounts, manage group memberships, configure RBAC roles, and set user permissions and authorizations.

provisioningScriptsTab.intro1: Shell scripts run top to bottom — drag to reorder. Paths are package-relative (like
provisioningScriptsTab.intro2: ); any script type, the shebang decides the interpreter; each entry runs on every walk. WHEN they run is where
provisioningScriptsTab.intro3: sits among this document's provisioning methods. Document
provisioningScriptsTab.intro4: reach scripts as environment variables under their exact names.

provisioningTransportTab.intro1: How the agent reaches the guest.
provisioningTransportTab.intro3: is for Windows guests — folder sync, ansible-local, and docker skip loudly over it, remote playbooks and scripts ride host-side ansible. Credentials stay the machine's SSH user/password (Machine Settings → Credentials).
provisioningTransportTab.oldSpellingIntro: This document also carries the old
provisioningTransportTab.oldSpellingRest: spelling of one or more of these keys — it stays verbatim (documents are never rewritten); the agents use the new spelling and narrate the shadowed key.

hostsYmlModal.footerExplanation: This YAML IS the whole document — removing a section DELETES it (sections: settings, zones, networks, disks, provisioner, metadata). Saved verbatim, key order kept, comments not preserved. The agent refuses unparseable YAML, impossible section shapes, bookkeeping keys, and unknown top-level keys; anything else questionable comes back as a non-blocking advisory.
hostsYmlModal.managerOnly: The machine document is manager-only — it carries provisioning variables and secrets your organization role cannot read.
